surfaceComplaint: There is a paparazzi drone near the yacht.
implications: The drone is not the real target; the footage is.
Action after useful visual contact may already be too late.
Destroying or disrupting the drone can be irrelevant, illegal, dangerous, or conspicuous.

possiblePepsis.name: Stop the drone
possiblePepsis.logic: Neutralize the aircraft before it captures anything useful.
possiblePepsis.rationale: This addresses the promoted problem in theory, but jamming, takedown, and interception collide with hard legal, safety, timing, and spectacle constraints.

possiblePepsis.name: Deny visual value
possiblePepsis.logic: Make the camera unable to capture a valuable private target.
possiblePepsis.rationale: This changes the win condition from controlling the drone to preventing viable footage.

possiblePepsis.name: Devalue footage after capture
possiblePepsis.logic: Make captured footage less valuable to buyers or platforms.
possiblePepsis.rationale: Plausible but weaker for this case because privacy has already failed once viable footage exists.

possiblePepsis.name: Raise capture or publication risk
possiblePepsis.logic: Increase legal, economic, or platform risk for operators and publishers.
possiblePepsis.rationale: May deter repeat attempts, but does not by itself prevent the immediate footage event.

tactics.name: Remove the target from frame
tactics.howItPursues: Move the protected person out of useful sightline before the drone reaches filming range.
hardConstraints: Requires detection before useful footage exists.
Fails if the protected person will not move.
softAxes: Discreet if the cue is private.

tactics.name: Block the frame
tactics.howItPursues: Use screens, blinds, glare, mist, lighting, or architecture to make the camera angle useless.
hardConstraints: Still must happen before useful footage exists.
softAxes: Hard to cover full altitude and angle space.

checks.label: Pipes carry water
checks.detail: The board exposes the complete reasoning chain from surface complaint to promoted problem to one surviving Pepsi and tactic split.

checks.label: Water not poisoned
checks.detail: The solved tactic is evaluator-side calibration. Do not move it into withheld prompts or generation context.

checks.label: Shower test
checks.detail: This is a single-Pepsi case; the board should not force fake plurality.
